LAW AND MOTION 301, A R G U E D AND THE COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE COURT SHOULD NOT FIND DEFT MITSAKOS IN CONTEMPT OF ORDER -- GRANTED PLAINTIFF AS TO ALL ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO JUDGE MCBRIDE'S 12/26/02 ORDER. PLAINTIFF IS TO SUBMIT A STATEMENT FOR ALL FEES AND COSTS UP TO UNTIL TODAY. COURT FURTHER ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT TO FORWARD ALL FINANCIAL MATERIAL FROM 1999-2002 TO PLAINTIFF NO LATER THAN NOON ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 1, 2003. THE COURT FURTHER DENIED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT. REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE COURT SHOULD NOT FIND DEFENDANT MITSAKOS IN CONTEMPT
NOTICE OF EXTENSION OF DEADLINE TO FILE MEMORANDUM OF COST AND MOTION FOR ATTYS' FEES FILED PLTF

LAW AND MOTION 301, MOTION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE COURT SHOULD NOT FIND DEFT MITSAKOS IN FURTHER CONTEMPT CONTINUED FROM JAN-14-2004 TO LAW AND MOTION AT FEB-13-2004, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301. MATTER IS CONTINUED. COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO FILE AND SERVE A STATUS REPORT NO LATER THAN 2/09/04. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTIONS FOR THE PERIOD FROM DECEMBER 26, 2002 TO SEPTEMBER 16, 2003 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS REASONABLY INCURRED TO INSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THIS COURT'S ORDERS FROM SEPTEMBER 17, 2003 TO DATE IS GRANTED ALTHOUGH THE PRECISE AMOUNT WILL ONLY BE DETERMIED UPON A PROPERLY SUPPORTED APPLICATION FOR THOSE FEES AND COSTS. MR. MITSAKOS IS ORDERED TO TURN OVER, WITHOUT EXCEPTION, THOSE DOCUMENTS ORDERED TO BE PRODUCED BY JUDGE MCBRIDE AND JUDGE WARREN IN THEIR ORDERS OF DECEMBER 26, 2002 AND SEPTEMBER 16, 2002, RESPECTIVELY; IF MR. MITSAKOS DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS, ABSENT GOOD CAUSE, THE COURT WILL TAKE WHATEVER STEPS ARE NECESSARY TO INSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS, INCLUDING CIVIL AND/OR CRIMINAL LIABILITY, FINANCIAL SANCTIONS, ISSUE PRECLUSION SANCTIONS, ETC. ATTORNEY WEXLER IS ORDERED TO ADVISE PLAINTIFF OF THE COURT'S RULING TODAY. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558
OPPOSITION MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL, FILED BY PLTF

NOTICE OF CONTINUATION OF HEARING REGARDING PLTF'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE COURT SHOULD NOT FIND DEFT MITSAKOS IN FURTHER CONTEMPT OF ORDER TO TURN OVER ALL BOOKS OF ACCOUNT AND RECORDS OF MIT

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT NICHOLAS MITSAKOS IS NOT PRESENT. PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE COURT SHOULD NOT FIND DEFENDANT MITSAKOS IN FURTHER CONTEMPT: HEARING HELD. PURSUANT TO CORPORATIONS CODE 17106(G), THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT NICHOLAS MITSAKOS HAS ENGAGED IN A WILLFUL AND INTENTIONAL REFUSAL TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS. ACCORDINGLY, 1) THE DEFENDANT NICHOLAS MITSAKOS PERSONALLY SHALL REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY FOR BRINGING THIS ACTION FOR ALL REASONABLE EXPENSES INCURRED INCLUDING ATTORNEY FEES; 2) THE CROSS COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS DISMISSED AS A SANCTION; AND 3) THE COURT ORDERS DEFENDANT NICHOLAS MITSAKOS TO BE PERSONALLY PRESENT ON MARCH 26, 2004 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301 THROUGH HIS COUNSEL, ATTORNEY ANDERSON, TO SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH ALL OF THE COURT'S ORDERS INCLUDING PAYMENT OF ALL ATTORNEY FEES ORDERED TO DATE. IF DEFENDANT NICHOLAS MITSAKOS HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS, JUDGMENT ON THE MERITS WILL BE ENTERED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. ATTORNEY CIRELLI TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER WHICH SHALL INCLUDE ALL ATTORNEY FEES PREVOUSLY ORDERED. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

LAW AND MOTION 301, ATTORNEY ANDERSON CONFIRMS THAT ALL ISSUES HAVE BEEN DISCUSSED WITH MR. MITSAKOS. THE COURT'S ORDER IS AS FOLLOWS:1) IN VIEW OF THE DISCOVERY ABUSE AND PURSUANT TO CCP 2023(B)(4)(D), THE COURT WILL SIGN, BUT NOT ENTER, A JUDGMENT BY DEFAULT, AS A TERMINATING SANCTION , AGAINST DEFENDANT MITSAKOS INDIVIDUALLY, AND IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY; 2) PURSUANT TO CORPORATIONS CODE 17106(G) OR, ALTERNATIVELY, CCP 2023(B)(1), THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF RESONABLE ATTORNEY FEES UP TO AND INCLUDING TODAY AS A FURTHER SANCTION RE ABUSE OF DISCOVERY; 3) MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR DEFENDANT AND STATUS CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED FOR 60 DAYS TO 05/24/04 IN DEPT. 301; ALL PARTIES AND THEIR COUNSEL ARE REQUIRED TO MAKE A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO PRODUCE EVERYTHING ORDERED BY THE COURT FROM 12/2002 TO DATE; 4) DEFENDANT MITSAKOS SHALL MAKE ALL PAYMENTS OF ATTORNEY FEES DUE WITHIN 60 DAYS (05/24/04); 5) THE COURT FURTHER MAKES AN AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES FROM TODAY FORWARD IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY AND THE AWARD IS ASSESSED AGAINST DEFENDANT MITSAKOS IN ADVANCE AND CONTINGENT UPON MITSAKOS' GOOD FAITH COOPERATION IN ATTEMPTING TO RESOLVE THIS MATTER ON THE MERITS. IF DEFENDANT MITSAKOS DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDERS,ABSENT GOOD CAUSE, THE JUDGMENT BY DEFAULT WILL BE ENTERED AND THE ADDITIONAL ATTORNEY FEES WILL BE ASSESSED. DEFENDANT MITSAKOS UNDERSTANDS AND ACCEPTS THE TERMS OF THE COURT'S ORDER AS STATED. DEF. MITSAKOS IS ORDERED TO BE PRESENT ON 05/24/04. COUNSEL WILL BE ALLOWED A 30 DAY CONTINUANCE TO 06/24/04 SHOULD ADDITIONAL TIME BE REQUIRED;IN THAT EVENT, DEFENDANT MITSAKOS IS ORDERED PRESENT. JUDGE: JAMES L. Motion For Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Find Deft Mitsakos In Further Contempt
Off Calendar Mar-26-2004 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kara M. Andersen, Esq., Keker & Van Nest LLP (415) 391-5400 Moving To Be Relieved As Counsel of Record for Defendant Mitsakos
Nicholas Mitsakos, Defendant
Lawrence M. Cirelli, Esq., Hanson Bridgett Marcus Vlahos Rudy LLP (415) 995-5003 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Patron Company
RULING: Attorney Anderson confirms that all issues have been discussed with Mr. Mitsakos. The Court's order is as follows:1) In view of the discovery abuse and pursuant to CCP 2023(b)(4)(d), the Court will sign, but not enter, a JUDGMENT BY DEFAULT, as a terminating sanction , against Defendant Mitsakos individually, and in favor of Plaintiff Patron Company; 2) Pursuant to Corporations Code 17106(g) or, alternatively, CCP 2023(b)(1), the Court GRANTS Plaintiff resonable attorney fees up to and including today as a further sanction re abuse of discovery; 3) Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel Of Record For Defendant and Status Conference is continued for 60 days to 05/24/04 in Dept. 301; all parties and their counsel are required to make a good faith effort to produce everything ordered by the Court from 12/2002 to date; 4) Defendant Mitsakos shall make all payments of attorney fees due within 60 days (05/24/04); 5) The Court further makes an award of reasonable attorney fees from today forward in favor of Plaintiff Patron Company and the award is assessed against Defendant Mitsakos in advance and contingent upon Mitsakos' good faith cooperation in attempting to resolve this matter on the merits. If Defendant Mitsakos does not comply with the Court's orders,absent good cause, the Judgment By Default will be entered and the additional attorney fees will be assessed. Defendant Mitsakos understands and accepts the terms of the Court's order as stated. Def. Mitsakos is ordered to be present on 05/24/04. Counsel will be allowed a 30 day continuance to 06/24/04 should additional time be required;in that event, Defendant Mitsakos is ordered present.(301/JLW/EH)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-10-2004 Continued To Jun-28-04 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Motion For Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Find Deft Mitsakos In Further Contempt
Off Calendar Feb-13-2004 Continued To Mar-26-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lawrence M. Cirelli, Esq., Hanson Bridgett Marcus Vlahos & Rudy LLP (415) 995-5003 for Plaintiff and Moving Party Patron Company
Kara M. Andersen, Esq., Keker & Van Nest LLP (415) 391-5400, for Defendant and Opposing Party Mitsakos
RULING:
Defendant Nicholas Mitsakos is not present. PLAINTIFF PATRON COMPANY'S Motion For Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Find Defendant Mitsakos In Further Contempt: HEARING HELD.
Pursuant to Corporations Code 17106(g), the Court finds that Defendant Nicholas Mitsakos has engaged in a willful and intentional refusal to comply with the Court's orders. Accordingly, 1) the Defendant Nicholas Mitsakos personally shall reimburse Plaintiff Patron Company for bringing this action for all reasonable expenses incurred including attorney fees; 2) the cross complaint for declaratory relief is dismissed as a sanction; and 3) the Court orders Defendant Nicholas Mitsakos to be personally present on March 26, 2004 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 301 through his Counsel, Attorney Anderson, to show compliance with all of the Court's orders including payment of all attorney fees ordered to date. If Defendant Nicholas Mitsakos has not complied with the Court's orders, judgment on the merits will be entered in favor of Plaintiff Patron Company as to all causes of action. Attorney Cirelli to prepare form of order which shall include all attorney fees prevously ordered. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

Motion For Order To Show Cause Why The Court Should Not Find Deft Mitsakos In Further Contempt
Off Calendar Jan-14-2004 Continued To Feb-13-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stacey L. Wexler, Esq., Keker & Van Nest LLP (415) 391-5400, for Defendant and Opposing Party Mitsakos Holdings LLC
RULING:
No other appearances on this matter. Matter is continued to 2/13/04 in Dept. 301; Counsel are ordered to file and serve a status report no later than 2/09/04.
Plaintiff's request for additional monetary sanctions for the period from December 26, 2002 to September 16, 2003 is DENIED.
Plaintiff's request for attorney fees and costs reasonably incurred to insure compliance with this Court's orders from September 17, 2003 to date is GRANTED although the precise amount will only be determied upon a properly supported application for those fees and costs.
Mr. Mitsakos is ordered to turn over, without exception, those documents ordered to be produced by Judge McBride and Judge Warren in their orders of December 26, 2002 and September 16, 2002, respectively; if Mr. Mitsakos does not comply with the Court's orders, absent good cause, the Court will take whatever steps are necessary to insure compliance with the Court's orders, including civil and/or criminal liability, financial sanctions, issue preclusion sanctions, etc.
Attorney Wexler is ordered to advise Plaintiff of the Court's ruling today.(301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
